lesson.menu_desc: Bargaining language, making offers and the second conditional -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Negotiating Terms -- Bargaining & the Second Conditional
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 18 -- Negotiating Terms

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel from the legal team, with a quick briefing before the negotiation you will lead tomorrow. The other side wants a lower price, but our bottom line is firm. Here is our strategy. If they asked for a discount, we would offer a longer contract instead, so we trade one thing for another. If we gave them flexible payment terms, they would probably accept the price. Remember, every concession needs something in return. Our leverage is the delivery date, because they need the goods quickly. If we reached a deadlock on price, we would move to the payment terms and look for a mutual solution. Stay calm, listen first, and aim for a deal that works for both sides. Good luck, and let me know how it goes.

extra_audio.text: First, both sides state what they want, and Simone explains the bottom line. Next, the other side makes an offer, and Simone studies it. Then Simone makes a counteroffer and asks for a concession in return, so each side trades something. After that, if they reach a deadlock, they move to a different point and look for a mutual solution. Finally, both sides agree on flexible terms and close the deal.
